reasoning: Taylor Swift's 'The Life of a Showgirl' debuted at #1 on the Billboard 200 on October 18, 2025. By its 5th week, its weekly consumption had already dropped to 120,000 equivalent album units. To achieve 18 consecutive weeks at #1, the album would need to hold the top spot seamlessly through mid-February 2026. This would require it to outsell the predictable surge of legacy Christmas albums (e.g., Michael Bublé's 'Christmas', which routinely hit 100,000+ units weekly in December and uniformly take the top chart positions). Furthermore, 18 consecutive weeks is an unprecedented streak for the modern era (for context, Swift's monumental 'The Tortured Poets Department' held for 12 consecutive weeks). Given the sales decay pattern and the absolute certainty of the holiday-music charting surge in December, resolving YES is essentially impossible. I set the probability to the floor value allowed (0.05).
